Title: Nicotiana benthamiana asparagine synthetase associates with IP-L and confers resistance against tobacco mosaic virus via the asparagine-induced salicylic acid signalling pathway

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces endogenous SA and abolishes Asn-induced resistance to TMV, demonstrating SA requirement for the metabolic defense effect
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Compromises Asn-induced antiviral defense by depleting SA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NahG transgenic plants failed to show Asn-induced resistance to TMV. 2. This supports that SA is required downstream of asparagine for antiviral defense.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful mechanistic tool gene to test SA dependence, but not a positive breeding target for resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic line pharmacological challenge with Asn; TMV-GFP imaging; RT-qPCR; western blot
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a mechanistic validation gene rather than a nutrient-pathway core catalyst or regulator

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Asparagine synthetase B; contains DUF3200, GATase 6, and GATase 7 domains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest expression in leaves; continuously induced by TMV infection; induced by MeSA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme for asparagine biosynthesis; rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Asparagine biosynthesis / nitrogen assimilation module linked to SA-mediated defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbAS-B physically associates with IP-L in yeast and in planta. 2. TMV infection increases NbAS-B transcript and protein accumulation. 3. NbAS-B silencing enhances TMV accumulation and spread, whereas overexpression suppresses TMV infection. 4. NbAS-B has asparagine synthetase activity and overexpression increases asparagine content. 5. NbAS-B promotes resistance through asparagine-associated activation of SA signaling, evidenced by effects on PR1/PR2 and loss of Asn-induced resistance in NahG plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes asparagine accumulation and enhances resistance to TMV infection via SA signaling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for transient expression/fusion constructs; PVX-mediated overexpression system
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major growth penalty or yield effect reported in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising metabolic engineering target to enhance antiviral resistance by increasing endogenous asparagine and SA-associated defense; suitable for transgenic overexpression or gene activation strategies, with potential combination with SA-defense traits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in leaves; continuously induced in systemic leaves upon TMV infection; induced after MeSA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct interaction between NbAS-B and TMV coat protein was detected; IP-L stabilizes NbAS-B likely by preventing ubiquitin/26S proteasome-mediated degradation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen transport/storage metabolite; in this study acts as an inducer of salicylic acid-mediated antiviral defense against TMV
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TMV infection; exogenous asparagine treatment; MeSA treatment; MG132 proteasome inhibitor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing; PVX-mediated overexpression; AS activity assay; HPLC quantification of asparagine; RT-qPCR; western blot; Y2H; Co-IP; LCI; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent transfer of an amide group from glutamine or ammonium to aspartate to produce asparagine

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Protein stability regulator / virus-interacting host factor
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Stabilizes NbAS-B protein accumulation; no direct transcriptional target genes demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NbAS-B accumulation but, when silenced alone, delays TMV infection; co-silencing with NbAS-B promotes TMV infection consistent with NbAS-B being dominant for the resistance phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Continuously induced in systemic leaves upon TMV infection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Acts as a proviral factor for TMV infection when considered alone, indicating possible tradeoff if manipulated without considering NbAS-B/defense context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for fusion constructs; PVX-mediated overexpression system
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct interaction with TMV coat protein was detected in this study, despite previous reports with ToMV CP; may have multiple functions in viral infection beyond SA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of asparagine accumulation through post-translational stabilization of NbAS-B rather than direct pathway catalysis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen transport/storage metabolite that here triggers SA-mediated antiviral defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; Co-IP; LCI; BiFC; co-expression stabilization assays; MG132 treatment; VIGS and overexpression phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IP-L is induced and stabilized during TMV infection. 2. IP-L physically interacts with NbAS-B in nucleus and cytosol. 3. IP-L increases NbAS-B protein accumulation by preventing 26S proteasome-mediated degradation. 4. Although IP-L alone promotes TMV infection, its interaction with NbAS-B links it to asparagine accumulation and defense-related SA signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CP-interacting protein-L; no canonical TF/enzyme role established in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Asparagine biosynthetic capacity via post-translational stabilization of NbAS-B
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein stabilization; inhibition of proteasome-mediated degradation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation requires caution because IP-L has dual roles: it stabilizes defense-associated NbAS-B yet also promotes TMV infection; not an ideal standalone engineering target
